title: The Mangled
wikitext:
{{Rewrite|date=april 12th, 2026|1= This page is not that great, and has many missed potential. I think if I'd candidate this for deletion, I'd be mostly speaking for myself, but the page is a tad bit forgettable in its current state. The entity's concept is great. The part where it misses is the execution. This page could definetly be made better with a narrative, or even a log that adds onto the page, or if it's clinical description would be made more sensory, and make you think much more instead of just trying to understand what it's saying. I hope this page could significantly improve if it were to be rewritten. -- [[User:Papashfh|Avian]] }} {{CSS|User:ReDcosmic/CharTotalVoided.css}}

{{DISPLAYTITLE: The Mangled}} {{Content Warning|bad_content=Body horror, violence|page=
'''Overview'''
{{Underline|FFFFFF}}
{{Underline|000000}}
[[File:Mangled.png|center|thumb|800x800px]]
'''TEST ID'''
The Mangled; Test Q
'''RESULT'''
Failure
'''STATUS'''
Escaped
'''Weaknesses'''
[[Object 12|Liquid Pain]]
'''EXTRA'''
Test Q has the hobby of role-playing with slain victims as family members. In this state, Test Q will undergo excesses shedding.
Test Q escaped the base located within [[Level 2]] and now missing. Volunteers who formed miniature search parties either came back empty handed, detained by [[Backrooms Investigation Foundation|B.I.F.]] or were killed by various factors. As for how the experiment escaped, Test Q appears to be greatly empowered by the black mould that grew on the ceiling of its cell as it grew in mass with large foliage like extensions to its mass. Test Q was created to be a counter against Class Psi- Mental Hazard levels by in taking the anomalous energy created by these levels. The idea was is, if Class Psi- Mental Hazard locations are discovered to spread their properties to other areas of the Backrooms, Test Q's species would be able to neutralise this threat while being able to produce pores that release chemicals capable of neutralising [[Object 12|Liquid Pain]] and [[Object 44|Cashew Water]]. Test Q can consume plants, vermin and fresh water. Test Q was still in early testing before it escaped so how it adapts to the environment and following interactions are unknown. Test Q originally looked like a normal person in facial features however, unlike a normal human it possessed flexibility with limbs, able to dislocate limbs, regenerate over 90% of its mass in the span of 15 hours, had fish hook-like spikes on its upper torso with digitigrade legs. These legs would help it spring to higher locations will the spikes act as booth protection and a way have material cling to the body. Due to need remove most of the small and large intestine, a modification was preformed. After destroying them mechanically and chemically using the stomach, the liver will absorb all nutrients, and waste will be vomited. Liquids will be filtered by the new liver also and it will go to the new heart to be dispersed into the body. {{Blurimage|file=mangledson.png|align=right|width=350px|caption=Picture of the Mangled shedding muscle and skin tissue, with exceptions for face, heart and bones.}} Test Q is '''not''' to be assigned a number as its transformation was affected by [[Phenomenon List|anomalous]] interferences. Tests were done to see if these accidental alterations would alter any variables. Behaviour became unstable with strange actions on killing. After slaying life forms, it will pretend that all victims are its relatives. As seen on the picture on the right, 2 wanderers were toyed with with Test Q acting like a child sleeping with both parents. In other cases, it pretended to be adults cleaning the house caused by children. When in this state, Test Q begins to shed heavily. It is theorised that this was caused by the interferences. It is believed that these traits kept as it escaped as many wanderers were found dead in Level 2 with the bodies either sitting and watching movies on screens found in their bases or sleeping peacefully in destroyed bloody rooms. The ground full of scales, puss and tissue. Test Q appears unaffected by pain caused by shedding yet screams on consuming Liquid Pain. Liquid Pain appears to either be unfilterable by its liver or it filters impurities in the substance thus making it stronger. Mould causes bizarre growths and current photographs show Test Q looking spider-like with it being able to regenerate these parts. Reproduction abilities are unclear and asexual cloning is uncertain however it shouldn't come surprising. It is unknown what can be used tame the subject. [[Object 4|Eye Fuel]] has only caused it better sight and it is unknown what interactions it may have and what results come across these tests. There is currently no public knowledge on Test Q with most being [[Backchat (Collaborative Log)|Backchat]] users talking about strange arrangement of dead bodies found in Level 2 and we must ensure that remains true as we do not need any more unwanted attention. }}


Written by [[User:Chaoscrafted|Kaos]].
